description_long: This is a military-style, twin-engine turboprop airplane with a high-wing configuration and a robust, utilitarian fuselage. The aircraft features a forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, and a prominent nose section. The wings are straight and extend laterally from the upper fuselage, each equipped with a propeller engine. The tail section is distinctive, featuring a twin vertical stabilizer (H-tail) and a horizontal stabilizer connecting the two fins. The landing gear appears to be tricycle-type, with visible main gear under the wings and a nose gear. The exterior is primarily gray with military insignia and orange highlights on the tail.
description: A gray, twin-propeller military airplane with a high-wing, H-tail design, and a forward, enclosed cockpit. Features include a robust fuselage, tricycle landing gear, and orange tail accents.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, high-mounted wings, twin propellers, and the H-tail with orange highlights.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the twin vertical stabilizers, horizontal stabilizer, and the width of the wingspan.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, showing the other side of the fuselage, propellers, and tail configuration.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the nose, cockpit windows, both propellers, and the straight high-wing layout.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turboprop engines mounted on the wings.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers and a connecting horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear, all appearing retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
